response: Artificial Intelligence In Medicine Analysis of Differential Intra-chromosomal Community Interactions: a Case Study of Breast Cancer --Manuscript Draft-- Manuscript Number: AIIM-D-23-02555 Article Type: Original research contributions Keywords: network clustering, intra-chromosomal community interaction, estrogen-treated tamoxifen-sensitive breast cancer cell line, Hi-C data, epigenetic data, integrative data analysis Manuscript Region of Origin: Europe Abstract: It is challenging to analyze dynamics of intra-chromosomal interactions when considering multiple high-dimensional epigenetic datasets. A computational integrative approach was proposed here to tackle the problem by integrating Hi-C data with other genomic data. A novel hyperparameter tuning method facilitating network clustering is developed to identify valid intra-chromosomal communities at different interaction resolutions. The approach was first trained on Hi-C data and other epigenetic data in an untreated and one hour estrogen (E2)-treated tamoxifen-sensitive (TS) breast cancer cell line, and revealed two major types of valid intra-chromosomal communities - active/repressive compartments, respectively, which resembles the properties of open/closed chromatin domains. It was further tested on and tamoxifen-resistant (TR) breast cancer cell line, and identified 515 differential interacting and differentially expressed genes (DIEGs) within intra-chromosomal community interactions. Further analysis of these DIEGs revealed that endocrine resistance is among the top biological pathways, suggesting a looping-mediated mechanism in regulating breast cancer tamoxifen resistance. This novel computational approach may have a broader application in many other biological systems for identifying a biological-specific differential community interaction.
